objective: Achieve 100% token resolution rate for content-ko A1 mapping pipeline. Current: 8.6% unresolved (494/5739 tokens).

parallel_groups.note: Dictionary, verb rules, and particle rules are independent — can run in parallel
Honorific chains and irregulars build on base rules

tasks.title: Filter Yarn metadata from tokenizer input
tasks.impact: Eliminates ~200 false unresolved tokens (40% of total unresolved)
tasks.goal: Stop counting Yarn node IDs, metadata lines, and English labels as Korean tokens.
tasks.analysis: The tokenizer currently processes Yarn node names (L16-D1-01, L25-D4-06), dialogue headings (hiking, lost-found, furniture), and English metadata (A1_L20_Start, demonstratives) as Korean tokens. These are not Korean text and should be filtered before tokenization.
actions: In run_mapping_pipeline.py extract_tokens_from_yarn(), add filters to skip lines that are: Yarn node declarations (starting with '==='), Yarn metadata (title:, tags:, position:), Yarn commands (<< and [[), node ID references matching pattern /^[A-Z0-9_-]+$/.
Add a regex filter for English-only tokens: skip any token matching /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/ (no Hangul characters).
Add a filter for Yarn speaker identifiers before the colon in dialogue lines.
Re-run pipeline and verify token count drops significantly. Target: total meaningful tokens < 4000.
acceptance: No English-only token appears in unresolved list.
No Yarn node ID (e.g. L16-D1-01) appears in unresolved list.
Pipeline still reports same resolved tokens for actual Korean tokens.
tasks.verification: python scripts/run_mapping_pipeline.py && grep -c 'L[0-9]' in unresolved should be 0

key_outcomes: Filtered Yarn metadata (title, tags, node IDs) from tokenizer.
Filtered English-only tokens (e.g. 'Korean', 'hiking') while preserving mixed tokens.
Fixed analyze_unresolved.py to read pipeline output provided accurate data.

tasks.title: Add missing dictionary nouns
tasks.impact: Resolves ~100 tokens (standalone nouns and proper nouns)
tasks.goal: Add all unresolved standalone nouns to mapping.json so dictionary_exact matches them.
tasks.analysis: Many simple nouns that appear without particles are unresolved because they are not in mapping.json. These are straightforward additions.
actions: Add the following nouns to content/source/ko/i18n/mapping.json (format: 'surface': 'ko:n:surface' or 'ko:prop:surface' for proper nouns).

Batch 1 — Common nouns: 멕시코, 청바지, 셔츠, 테이블, 메이커, 샐러드, 아이스티, 스터디, 스터디룸, 카운터, 열쇠, 탁자, 정류장, 동아리방, 욕조, 부엌, 침실, 접시, 숟가락, 드링크, 밴드, 뷔페, 팬케이크, 파스타, 오믈렛, 결혼식, 박물관, 쇼핑몰, 헬스장, 어학원, 우체국, 은행, 대학교, 호텔, 재킷, 별거, 보충, 보람, 수고, 기간, 이상, 점수, 외모, 연세, 거리, 동네, 위치, 샤워, 부스, 기본적, 외국어, 스페인어, 일본어, 프랑스어, 태국어, 경영학, 한자, 지우개, 종이, 모두, 겨우, 꽤, 엄청, 열심히, 에이, 세상, 소, 닭, 별
Batch 2 — Proper nouns: 소라, 로라, 케이티, 조
Batch 3 — Derived/compound forms: DJ (as ko:n:DJ), 007 (skip — numeric title)
acceptance: All listed nouns appear in mapping.json.
Pipeline re-run shows these nouns resolved by dictionary_exact rule.
No new schema validation errors.

tasks.title: Add verb conjugation rules
tasks.goal: Add suffix rules for missing verb ending patterns.
tasks.analysis: Multiple verb conjugation patterns are unresolved because we lack rules for certain suffix combinations.
actions: Add rules to content-ko/scripts/core/rules/30_verb_endings.json for the following patterns:
1. verb_go_connective: (.+)고$ → [ko:v:{stem}, ko:e:고] — Connective ending (공부하고, 끝나고, 착하고, 크고, 앉고, 일하고, 완벽했고)
4. verb_eosseoyo_past_polite: (.+)었어요$ → [ko:v:{stem}, ko:e:었, ko:e:어요] — Past polite (어려웠어요, 멀었어요, 앉았어요 etc.)
5. verb_asseoyo_past_polite: (.+)았어요$ → [ko:v:{stem}, ko:e:았, ko:e:어요] — Past polite (갔어요, 않았어요)
6. verb_neundeyo_background: (.+)는데요$ → already exists but duplicated; verify priority
acceptance: All listed patterns resolve correctly with pipeline.
Reconstruction check passes for each new rule.
Add test cases to tests/rule_tests.json for each new rule.
python tests/run_rule_tests.py passes.

tasks.title: Add particle combination rules
tasks.goal: Add rules for missing particle suffixes and particle chain patterns.
tasks.analysis: Many N+particle combos fail because we lack rules for certain particles and particle chains.
actions: 4. particle_hante: (.+)한테$ → [ko:n:{stem}, ko:p:한테] — Indirect (씨한테나 — needs compound rule)
6. noun_eseo_neun: already exists (priority 46). Verify coverage of 에서는 pattern.

7. Add numeric counter rules to 20_regex_time.json: (\d+)시간$ for time duration (2시간), (\d+)층에$ for floor (2층에), (\d+)달러예요$ for price copula (10달러예요), (\d+)문제$ for problem count (20문제), (\d+)분밖에$ for minutes-only (5분밖에), (\d+)호에$ for room number (213호에), (\d+)호예요$ for room copula (216호예요), (\d+)세세요$ for age honorific (47세세요)
acceptance: 학교까지, 5분밖에, 은행하고, 2시간, 2층에, 213호에 all resolve.
Test cases added to tests/rule_tests.json.
python tests/run_rule_tests.py passes.

tasks.title: Add honorific and auxiliary verb chain rules
tasks.impact: Resolves ~30 tokens (complex verb chains with honorific markers)
tasks.goal: Handle V-시- (honorific), V-아/어 주다 (benefactive), and V-아/어 드리다 (humble benefactive) chains.
tasks.analysis: Forms like 도와드릴까요, 잘하시겠어요, 친절하시고요, 일하시는데요 require multi-morpheme parsing that combines base verb + honorific marker + ending.
actions: 10. verb_hago_hada_connective: (.+)하고$ — Ambiguous! Could be N+하고 (comitative) or V-하+고 (connective). Use dictionary_required: true to disambiguate.
acceptance: 도와드릴까요, 잘하시겠어요, 친절하시고요 resolve correctly.
No regression in existing rule_tests.json tests.
Test cases added for each new rule.

tasks.title: Add irregular verb and adjective forms
tasks.goal: Add rules and dictionary entries for irregular verb/adjective conjugations that don't follow standard patterns.
tasks.analysis: Korean has multiple irregular verb classes. Key unresolved irregulars: 켜다→켜져/켤까요 (regular but missing), 꺼지다→꺼져, 어렵다→어려웠어요 (ㅂ-irregular), 가깝다→가까운/가깝네요 (ㅂ-irregular), 멀다→멀지 (ㄹ-irregular), 불편하다→불편해요 (regular hada), 오래되다→오래된 (compound).
actions: Add to mapping.json: 켜다, 꺼지다, 켜지다, 가깝다, 불편하다, 오래되다, 달리다(to be attached), 걸리다(to take time), 않다(negation), 나쁘다
Add to lemmatizer.py irregulars dict: 어려워→어렵다, 가까워→가깝다, 가까운→가깝다, 달려→달리다, 걸려→걸리다
Add hardcoded irregular rules to 10_irregulars.json if standard rules cannot handle: 그래(→그러다), 그랬어요(→그러다+past), 어때(→어떻다), 저런(→저렇다+ㄴ)
Verify that ㅂ-irregular generic rule (verb_woyo_irregular) handles new entries via lemmatizer
acceptance: 어려웠어요, 가까운, 켜져, 꺼져, 켤까요, 그래, 어때 all resolve.
Lemmatizer returns correct 다-form for all new irregulars.
Test cases added.

tasks.title: Final sweep and 0% gate verification
tasks.impact: Closes any remaining gaps and verifies 0% unresolved
tasks.goal: Run pipeline, identify any remaining unresolved tokens, fix them, and verify 100% resolution.
actions: For each remaining unresolved token, determine category and fix (dictionary entry, rule, or tokenizer filter).
Verify QA report shows 0.0% unresolved ratio.
Update handoff.stage-02.json with final gate results.
Run: python tests/run_rule_tests.py to confirm no regressions.
acceptance: QA report shows unresolved ratio = 0.0%.
All rule tests pass.
handoff.stage-02.json updated with final gate results.
analyze_unresolved.py shows 0 unresolved tokens.
tasks.verification: python tests/run_rule_tests.py && python scripts/run_mapping_pipeline.py && python scripts/analyze_unresolved.py

gemini_prompt_template: 請讀取 KO_RESOLUTION_100PCT_TASKS.json，執行任務 {TASK_ID}。工作對象在 content-ko 資料夾。完成後請用 reporting_format 回報，附上可重現命令與測試結果。每完成一個任務後執行 python tests/run_rule_tests.py && python scripts/run_mapping_pipeline.py 驗證。
notes: KO-RES-01 should be done FIRST — it dramatically reduces noise in the unresolved list.
After KO-RES-01, re-run analyze_unresolved.py to get a clean list for KO-RES-02~06.
Always clear cache (auto-invalidation handles this) and run tests after changes.
The analyze_unresolved.py script shows ALL unresolved tokens, not just top 20.
Rules are split into category files in scripts/core/rules/. Edit the appropriate category file.
